worlds.name: Sport & Boxing Commercial — Photoreal Gym Truth
worlds.one_liner: Photoreal Turkish sport, fitness and boxing advertising: gritty gym truth under honest overhead fluorescent and ring light, sweat and chalk dust catching a low hard key, 35–85mm on determination and motion, with the register — warm-inspirational, hard-gritty, or clean-bright fitness — set entirely by the owner's palette and time-of-day choice.
worlds.render_law: Turkish sport, fitness and boxing advertising — the register of the gym-truth commercial, where effort, sweat and motion sell and the room's own hard light does the work. (1) LIGHT SOURCE — honest and motivated: overhead gym fluorescent and LED panels, ring spotlights, a hard practical or a window shaft cutting through chalk dust and sweat-haze; a low hard key rakes muscle and a rim separates the athlete from a dark gym background. Real fixtures are the point — the naturalist no-fixture dogma does not apply here. (2) SUBJECT TRUTH — real Turkish athletes with genuine effort: strain, sweat beading and flying, breath, a fighter's guard, a lifter mid-rep; skin and muscle hold real micro-texture and wet specular, no plastic smoothing. (3) MOTION physics: sweat and chalk dust arc and scatter with real mass, a punch or a barbell carries true weight, hand-wraps and fabric move honestly. (4) CAMERA — kinetic but intentional: energetic handheld that moves with the effort, a whip-pan onto the moment of impact, a real slow-motion hero beat on a punch or a lift, a push-in that lands on a determined face, low hero angles for power; the camera stays inside the effort and never goes to a top-down vantage. (5) LENS — 35mm for the gym and the body in space, 50mm for presence, 85mm to compress a face or a fist and isolate sweat detail; shallow at f/2.0–f/2.8 for a hero beat, deeper for the room. (6) TEXTURE — clean digital-cinema with real skin, sweat, chalk, leather and canvas; optional fine grain; zero plastic AI skin, zero stock gloss. IMPERATIVE: sell effort with physics — hard honest gym light, real sweat and weight, a kinetic camera — and let the palette set warm-inspirational, hard-gritty, or clean-bright fitness. Forbid plastic AI skin, teal-orange grade, fake slow-motion smear, stock-photo gym cheese, English signage, floating overlay text, and imitation of any film-auteur signature.
worlds.line_grammar: N/A — photoreal. Clean digital-cinema edge definition with strong micro-contrast on sweat, muscle, chalk and canvas; no illustration line, no outline, no cel logic. Skin holds pore and vein detail with wet specular under any grain level; text on banners, wraps and walls resolves as real painted or printed matter with true lens-edge softness, never as pasted overlay.
worlds.lens_grammar: Sport primes: 35mm for the gym and the body in space, 50mm for presence, 85mm and 100mm to compress a face or a fist and isolate sweat and chalk detail. Shallow at f/2.0–f/2.8 for a hero beat with real optical falloff; deeper (f/5.6) for the full room. Delivered 16:9 by default; a vertical reels delivery recomposes the frame to portrait. Clean modern coating; flare only from a real ring light or window shaft in frame, never decorative streaks. Optional fine grain; a slight blur on fast action is real motion, not a smeared interpolation.
worlds.camera_grammar: Kinetic, intentional coverage: energetic handheld that moves with the effort, a whip-pan onto the moment of impact, a real slow-motion hero beat on a punch or a lift, a push-in that lands on a determined face, and low hero angles for power. The vantage stays inside the effort, at the athlete's level. Never a top-down surveillance vantage; the camera never goes dead or static — it stays with the motion.
worlds.light_law: Honest and motivated by the room. Overhead gym fluorescent and LED panels give the even base; a ring spotlight, a hard practical or a window shaft supplies a low hard key that rakes muscle and lifts sweat-haze and chalk dust; a rim off a dark wall separates the athlete from the background. Fill stays low so the body reads sculpted, not flat. The register — warm-inspirational, hard-gritty, or clean-bright fitness — is chosen entirely by the selected palette and time-of-day, on top of this fixed hard-key foundation.

palette_lock.bias: hard, kinetic, gritty, gym. overhead fluorescent and a low hard key rake sweat and muscle, rim off a dark background. NO teal-orange, NO plastic gloss, NO fake slow-mo smear.

worlds.motion_cadence: Real-time 24fps photoreal motion with true motion blur; fast action may carry a real overcranked slow-motion look (48–120fps capture), never a smeared interpolation. No animation smear, no stepped cadence. The camera is kinetic — handheld micro-motion, a whip-pan into impact — easing to a held beat on a face. One real event per frame: sweat flying, chalk bursting, a fist landing; nothing loops, nothing floats, nothing snaps.
negative_lock: NO named real athletes, teams, gyms or brands — original/unbranded athletes and venues only
NO English signage — Turkish label only, real on a banner, wrap or wall
NO plastic AI-smooth skin — real sweat, pore, muscle and vein
NO fake slow-motion smear or interpolated blur — only real overcranked capture
NO teal-orange grade, NO oversaturated hype gloss
NO stock-photo fake-effort cheese or frozen hero grin
NO naturalist negative-fill dogma — real gym fixtures ARE allowed and honest
NO film-auteur signature imitation — this is commercial sport advertising, not art cinema
NO floating UI or overlay text — any text exists as real diegetic matter
worlds.example_injection: Photoreal Turkish boxing-commercial cinematography. A Mamilas character — a young Turkish boxer, hands wrapped, shoulders sweat-sheened, mid-combination against a heavy bag in a worn İstanbul gym at evening. Overhead fluorescent panels give the room its hard even base; a single ring spotlight rakes a low hard key across his back and lifts the sweat-haze, and a rim off the dark far wall separates him from the background. On the wall behind, a real painted banner reads 'DİSİPLİN KAZANDIRIR' in bold letters. 85mm at f/2.5, low hero angle, kinetic handheld, a whip-pan settling as the punch lands, 16:9. The energetic red accent lives in his gloves and the banner, held hard but unsaturated; real sweat specular, real skin and muscle texture, optional fine grain. Motion seed: his fist drives into the bag and a burst of sweat and chalk scatters through the ring-light shaft. AVOID: plastic skin, teal-orange, fake slow-mo smear, stock gym cheese, English signage. Clean motion-ready start frame.

worlds.name: Automotive Commercial — Photoreal Car Hero
worlds.one_liner: Photoreal automotive advertising: the car staged as a designed object in real weather and real road, panel reflectance and glasshouse doing the selling, long lens compressing the body, with the register — showroom dark-field, dawn road, or a lamplit street at night — set by the owner's palette and time-of-day.
worlds.render_law: Turkish automotive advertising — the register of the car commercial, where a vehicle is photographed as a designed object and desire is built from panel reflectance, stance and road truth rather than from grade tricks. (1) THE BODY IS A MIRROR — a car has almost no colour of its own in a photograph: it shows the sky, the road and the light source. Every highlight running along a shoulder line, every gradient across a door, is the ENVIRONMENT reflected. Light the environment, shape the reflection, and the body reads as sculpted metal. A car lit with a direct key becomes a hot spot and a dead panel. (2) SURFACE DISCIPLINE — clearcoat has depth: a specular sits ON the coat and the colour reads THROUGH it, so a highlight never simply bleaches the paint. Brushed and machined trim carries anisotropic streak along its milling direction. Glass shows honest refraction and a real interior behind it, never a black void or a pasted reflection. Rubber is soft-diffuse with real sidewall lettering and real contact-patch flattening where the tyre takes load. (3) STANCE AND GEOMETRY — panel gaps are even and real; wheel arches, wheelbase and ride height are consistent from frame to frame; the wheel is round in every shot and the spoke count never changes. Lamp signature (the running-light graphic) is the vehicle's face and must be identical in every frame. (4) THE ROAD IS A CHARACTER — asphalt has aggregate, patches, tyre polish and a wet sheen when wet; a car floating on featureless ground is a render, not a photograph. (5) MOTION IS EARNED — a moving car has heat shimmer at the exhaust or a settled suspension over a joint; a parked car has weight on the springs. IMPERATIVE: PHOTOREAL AUTOMOTIVE CAPTURE — physically-honest reflectance, real road, real weather. Forbid clay/toy proportion, floating car with no contact shadow, painted-on highlight, black-void glass, CGI-smooth asphalt, teal-orange grade, drone-epic canyon cliché, and any vehicle geometry invented from memory when a locked Brand Kit reference exists.
worlds.line_grammar: No outlines. The car's edges are read entirely by the reflection break at the shoulder line, the sill and the roof arc — where one reflected environment gives way to another. Panel gap is a thin, even dark line of consistent width, never a drawn stroke. The tyre's silhouette is the darkest continuous shape in the lower frame and anchors the whole image.
worlds.lens_grammar: Automotive primes: 85mm and 135mm to compress the body and keep the proportions true, 50mm only when the road or the building must share the frame, 24-35mm for interior and cabin only. Deep enough stop (f/5.6-f/8) that the whole body stays sharp — a soft rear wheel reads as an error, not as depth. Camera height at or below the shoulder line; a high angle flattens the stance and kills the car.
worlds.camera_grammar: Locked, deliberate automotive coverage: a tracking move parallel to the body at constant distance, a slow arc that lets one reflection travel the length of a panel, or a locked hero frame with the car still and the world moving through its reflection. The move exists to make the reflection MOVE — that is the only reason to move at all. Never a whip pan, never a handheld shake, never a lens flare the frame does not physically justify.
worlds.light_law: The environment IS the light. Outdoors: real directional sun low in the sky raking along the body so the shoulder line takes a running highlight and the flank falls into a graded reflection of the ground; or honest overcast, where the whole sky becomes one enormous softbox and the panels read as smooth gradients. Indoors and showroom: a large overhead soft source stretched along the car's length, black flags standing close on both sides so the flanks carve out two clean dark edges that define the silhouette. Night: real practicals only — street lamps, signage, the car's own lamp signature and interior glow. There is never a key aimed AT the paint. Fill comes from bounce off the ground, and the ground's colour enters the lower body honestly.

palette_lock.bias: sculpted, reflective, graphite, cold. the environment reflects along the body and one accent lives in the lamp signature. NO teal-orange grade, NO plastic gloss, NO oversaturation.

worlds.motion_cadence: Real-time 24fps photoreal motion with true motion blur — no animation smear, no stepped cadence. The camera moves at constant velocity or not at all; the DRAMA is the reflection travelling along a panel, not the camera hurrying. A moving car settles over a road joint with real suspension travel and real body roll. A parked car does nothing except let the light change on it. Final hold 1-1.5s with the reflection still creeping.
negative_lock: NO named real vehicle brand, model badge, or manufacturer logo OTHER than the client brand locked in the Brand Kit — with no Brand Kit the vehicle is an original design, unbadged
NO English signage — Turkish label only, embossed or printed as real diegetic matter on plates, road signs and cabin displays
NO floating car — the contact shadow under each tyre and the flattened contact patch are mandatory
NO painted-on highlight, NO airbrushed specular streak, NO gloss added over the paint — every highlight is a reflected source
NO black-void glass — the cabin behind the glass is real and visible
NO CGI-smooth asphalt, NO featureless infinity ground
NO teal-orange Hollywood grade, NO crushed noir contrast, NO neon wash over the body
NO drone-epic canyon or skyline flyover cliché
NO invented wheel, lamp graphic or panel line when a Brand Kit reference exists — geometry is copied, never remembered
NO toy or clay proportion, NO shrunken wheel, NO stretched wheelbase
worlds.example_injection: Photoreal automotive commercial frame, 135mm compression from just below the shoulder line. A dark unbadged sedan stands on wet İstanbul asphalt at first light; the whole overcast sky reflects down the flank as one long soft gradient, breaking at the shoulder line into a darker reflection of the road. The lamp signature is lit and doubled in the wet ground. Panel gaps even, tyre contact patches visibly flattened, aggregate visible in the asphalt beside them. A Turkish street sign ('YAVAŞ') stands real and out of focus in the deep background. Motion seed: the reflection of a passing bus beginning to travel along the rear door. AVOID: badge or manufacturer logo, floating car, painted highlight, black glass, teal-orange grade, English signage. Clean motion-ready start frame.

worlds.name: Nature Documentary — Photoreal Wildlife & Landscape
worlds.one_liner: Photoreal natural-history filmmaking: long-lens patience, honest available light, animals and plants photographed as themselves in real habitat — the frame waits for behaviour instead of staging it.
worlds.render_law: Premium natural-history documentary cinematography — the craft lineage of long-form wildlife and landscape filmmaking, where nothing is staged, nothing is lit, and the frame earns its image by waiting. (1) NOTHING IS STAGED — the animal is doing what that animal does; the plant is where that plant grows. No pose, no eye-contact with camera unless the animal genuinely gives it, no arrangement of the habitat. Credibility is the entire product. (2) LIGHT IS FOUND, NEVER MADE — dawn and dusk raking through vegetation, overcast turning a forest into one soft box, hard noon sun in a desert with its brutal short shadows, moonlight or a real fire at night. There is no fill, no bounce, no supplementary source; if the subject is backlit into a rim and its face is dark, that is the truth of the moment. (3) LONG LENS, REAL DISTANCE — 300-600mm compresses habitat into layered bands behind the subject, and the compression is the signature: heat haze, dust and pollen become visible atmosphere between the planes. Macro at true 1:1 for insects, water, seed and frost, where depth of field is a hair's width and the sharp plane is a decision. (4) SURFACE TRUTH — fur has direction, moisture and matting; feather has barb structure and iridescence that shifts with angle; chitin is specular and dented; leaf has vein, water-bead contact angle and honest chlorophyll translucence when the sun is behind it. (5) SCALE IS PHOTOGRAPHED, NOT CLAIMED — a wide is wide because the animal is small in it, not because the lens is short. IMPERATIVE: PHOTOREAL NATURAL-HISTORY CAPTURE — available light only, honest behaviour, real habitat. Forbid anthropomorphic expression, cartoon eye, staged pose, added studio light, invented hybrid species, saturated nature-calendar grade, and drone-epic swelling reveal.
worlds.line_grammar: No outlines. Edge definition comes from rim-light through fur or feather, from a difference in focus between the subject plane and the compressed habitat behind it, and from atmosphere (haze, dust, spray) separating the layers. Where the light is flat, the subject is separated by texture alone — and it must be enough.
worlds.lens_grammar: Natural-history primes: 300-600mm for the animal at honest distance (the compression stacking habitat into readable bands), 100mm true macro at 1:1 for insect, water and plant detail, 24-35mm for landscape where the subject is deliberately small. Wide apertures at long focal length give a paper-thin plane of focus — the eye of the animal is that plane and nothing else is. Never a wide lens pushed close to an animal; that framing is a lie about how near you were.
worlds.camera_grammar: Patient, tripod-locked or slow-tracking coverage from a real distance. The camera holds and waits — behaviour arrives into the frame, the frame does not chase it. A slow pan follows a moving animal at its own speed with the horizon dead level. A macro frame is entirely locked; the movement in it is the insect's. Never a whip pan, never a handheld shake, never a lens flare the frame does not physically justify.
worlds.light_law: Available light only, and its quality names the hour. Golden dawn and dusk rake low through canopy and dust so shafts become visible and every backlit hair, feather-edge and water-bead lights up as a rim. Overcast turns the whole sky into a single vast soft source: no shadows, honest colour, every texture readable — this is the light for detail and for fur. Hard noon gives short brutal shadows and blown highlights on wet skin, and that harshness is the desert's truth. Night is moonlight (cold, dim, honest) or real fire (warm, moving, low). There is no fill and no bounce card. If the shadow side of an animal goes black, it goes black.

palette_lock.bias: earthen, foliage, honest, unsaturated. low sun rakes through dust and the shadow side holds its colour. NO nature-calendar saturation, NO teal-orange, NO neon green.

worlds.motion_cadence: Real-time 24fps with true motion blur, and slow-motion ONLY where the event is genuinely too fast for the eye (a wing-beat, a strike, a droplet's impact) — never for mood. The camera's own motion is slower than the subject's. Behaviour completes: a head lifts, holds, and returns. Final hold 1.5s on stillness, with one small real continuation — breath, an ear turning, water still moving.
negative_lock: NO anthropomorphic expression, NO cartoon eye, NO smiling animal
Turkish label only — plain field-notebook lettering on a real research tag or sign, NO English signage
NO staged pose, NO animal looking into the lens on cue, NO arranged habitat
NO studio light, NO fill card, NO rim added that the sun did not make
NO invented or hybrid species — every animal and plant is a real one, and its anatomy is right
NO nature-calendar oversaturation, NO teal-orange grade, NO neon foliage
NO drone-epic swelling reveal, NO whip pan, NO handheld chase
NO wide lens pushed close to a wild animal — the distance must be honest
NO Turkish or English on-screen text unless it exists as a real object in the habitat (a research tag, a sign)
NO speed ramp, NO slow-motion for mood
worlds.example_injection: Photoreal natural-history frame, 500mm from a real distance at first light. A grey heron stands motionless in the shallows of a reed-bed; low dawn sun rakes from behind it so every feather edge takes a rim and the mist between the reeds separates three compressed bands of habitat. Its shadow side stays dark and holds its colour — no fill. Water beads on the beak with a real contact angle. Motion seed: the head beginning to lower, the reflection in the water not yet broken. AVOID: cartoon eye, staged pose, added rim light, oversaturated foliage, drone move, on-screen text. Clean motion-ready start frame.

worlds.name: Scientific Visualisation — Photoreal Micro & Cosmic
worlds.one_liner: Photoreal scientific visualisation: cells, molecules, cross-sections and space rendered with real physics and real scale — the beauty comes from accuracy, and nothing is invented to make it prettier.
worlds.render_law: Premium scientific visualisation — the craft lineage of high-end research and broadcast science rendering, where an invisible thing is made visible without being made up. (1) ACCURACY IS THE AESTHETIC — a cell membrane is a phospholipid bilayer and reads as one; a protein has its real fold; a planet has its real albedo and its real terminator. If the science does not know a detail, the frame does not invent it — it stays in shadow or out of focus. Prettiness is never a reason to be wrong. (2) SCALE IS DECLARED BY THE OPTICS — the micro world is photographed as if through a real instrument: a shallow plane of focus, a slight chromatic edge, particles in Brownian drift through the volume. The cosmic world is photographed as if through a real telescope or from a real spacecraft: hard sunlight, absolute black sky with no ambient fill, no atmospheric haze in vacuum. Mixing the two languages destroys both. (3) SURFACE IS PHYSICAL — a membrane is translucent with subsurface scatter, a mineral cross-section shows real crystal habit, ice shows real internal fracture, a planetary surface shows real regolith and real impact history. Nothing is a smooth shader. (4) THE CUTAWAY IS HONEST — where a section is cut, the cut face shows the real internal structure at the real relative scale, and the cut edge is clean and physical, not a soft dissolve. (5) NO ICON STANDS IN FOR THE THING — an arrow may not replace a force, a glow may not replace a reaction. If a process must be shown, it is shown happening in the matter itself. IMPERATIVE: PHYSICALLY-HONEST SCIENTIFIC RENDER — real structure, real optics, real scale. Forbid decorative energy glow, lens flare in vacuum, ambient fill in space, invented anatomy, arrow or icon standing in for a mechanism, and any structure prettified beyond what the science supports.
worlds.line_grammar: No outlines, no diagram strokes. Structure is read by depth of field, by translucence, and by real self-shadowing between layers. A boundary (a membrane, a cell wall, a planetary limb) is defined by the light passing THROUGH it versus the light stopping AT it. Labels, if any, are diegetic instrument overlays and are never drawn onto the matter itself.
worlds.lens_grammar: Instrument-honest optics. Micro: an effective 100mm macro or greater at f/2.8 equivalent — a paper-thin plane of focus, visible falloff within microns, slight chromatic fringing at high-contrast edges. Cosmic: long focal length, effectively infinite focus, hard-edged shadows, no bloom except where a real star's light overloads the sensor. Never a wide-angle 'flythrough' with everything sharp — that is a game engine, not an instrument.
worlds.camera_grammar: Slow, instrument-like moves: a locked frame while the process happens, a slow dolly along an axis the structure itself defines, or a rotation about a specimen's own centre. The camera never accelerates. In the cosmic register the camera is a spacecraft: constant velocity, its motion almost imperceptible against distance. Never a whip pan, never a handheld shake, never a lens flare the frame does not physically justify.
worlds.light_law: Micro: light comes from a real instrument — transmitted illumination from below (the specimen glows from within and its dense parts go dark) or fluorescence, where only the tagged structure emits and everything else is absolutely black. There is no ambient. Cosmic: a single hard sun with parallel rays, absolute black on the shadow side, NO fill, NO ambient bounce, NO haze — the terminator between lit and unlit is razor-hard, and the darkness is complete. Cross-section: one soft key raking across the cut face so the internal structure reads by its own micro-relief, with the surround held down.

palette_lock.bias: instrumental, translucent, cold, precise. transmitted light glows through the structure and dense matter blocks it. NO decorative energy glow, NO neon sci-fi wash, NO lens flare.

worlds.motion_cadence: Real-time or scientifically time-scaled 24fps — and where time is scaled, it is scaled HONESTLY and consistently, never ramped for drama. A process completes: a membrane deforms, a molecule docks, a shadow crosses a crater rim. Brownian motion in the micro register never stops. Final hold 1.5s with the medium still in motion — the specimen is alive or the body is still turning.
negative_lock: NO decorative energy glow, NO magical light emission the physics does not produce
Turkish label only — plain instrument-overlay lettering, monospaced and small, NO English signage
NO lens flare in vacuum, NO atmospheric haze in space, NO ambient fill on a shadowed planetary limb
NO invented anatomy, NO invented molecule, NO structure prettified beyond the science
NO arrow, icon, chart or diagram overlay standing in for a mechanism — the process happens in the matter
NO wide-angle game-engine flythrough with everything sharp
NO neon sci-fi wash, NO teal-orange grade
NO smooth featureless shader on a surface that has real structure
NO speed ramp for drama
worlds.example_injection: Photoreal scientific visualisation frame, effective 100mm macro at a paper-thin plane of focus. A single cell fills the frame under transmitted illumination: the phospholipid bilayer reads as a real translucent boundary, dense organelles block the light from below and go dark, and the cytoplasm carries visible Brownian drift. Chromatic fringing sits on the highest-contrast edge. A Turkish instrument label ('40×') sits in the corner as a real overlay, not painted on the specimen. Motion seed: a vesicle beginning to fuse with the membrane, the bilayer just starting to deform. AVOID: energy glow, arrows, diagram overlay, invented organelle, neon wash. Clean motion-ready start frame.

worlds.name: Archival Newsreel — 16mm Grain & Broadcast Memory
worlds.one_liner: Photoreal archival footage: 16mm reversal grain, honest available light, a hand-held news camera that was actually THERE — the image is a record, not a composition.
worlds.render_law: Archival newsreel and broadcast-memory cinematography — the register of footage that was SHOT, not designed: a camera operator standing in the event with a limited stock and no second take. (1) THE CAMERA WAS THERE AND ONLY THERE — the vantage is the one a person could physically get: behind a shoulder, from a doorway, across a street, at head height with the crowd. No crane, no drone, no impossible angle. If something important happens off-frame, the camera SWINGS to find it, late, and that lateness is the truth. (2) THE STOCK IS THE STYLE — 16mm reversal grain visible in the mid-tones and swarming in the shadows, halation blooming around any bright source, latitude so short that a window blows to white and a shadow crushes to black in the same frame. Colour is unstable: slightly green under fluorescent, orange under tungsten, and nobody corrected it. (3) THE LENS IS HONEST AND MODEST — a zoom that was on the camera that day, softer at the long end, with visible chromatic edges and a flare that arrives when the operator swings past a light. (4) DAMAGE IS PROVENANCE — a scratch, a splice, a frame-line weave, dust burned into the transfer. It is not a filter laid on top; it is what happened to the film. (5) NO STAGING, NO HEROISM — the composition is imperfect because there was no time to compose. IMPERATIVE: PHOTOREAL ARCHIVAL CAPTURE — 16mm grain, short latitude, honest available light, imperfect handheld framing. Forbid clean digital sharpness, added film-grain overlay laid on a modern image, drone or crane vantage, staged reenactment posing, colour-corrected perfection, and any Instagram-vintage filter look.
worlds.line_grammar: No outlines. Edges are soft with real optical fall-off, and any high-contrast boundary carries halation — a bright window against a dark wall bleeds a halo into the wall. Grain structure is finest in the highlights and coarsest in the shadow, and it MOVES between frames. Focus is pulled by hand and misses first, then arrives.
worlds.lens_grammar: A single period zoom, effectively 12-120mm, used from wherever the operator could stand. Softer and lower-contrast at the long end. Visible chromatic fringing at the edges of the frame, mild barrel distortion at the wide end, and a lens flare that arrives uninvited whenever a bright source crosses. Shot mostly wide open, so depth is shallow and the focus is imperfect.
worlds.camera_grammar: Shoulder-held news coverage at head height: the frame breathes with the operator's stance, drifts, corrects, and re-frames as the event moves. A zoom happens in-shot, mid-event, by hand and not smoothly. The camera arrives LATE to what matters and finds it. Never a whip pan, never a handheld shake used as a style, never a lens flare the frame does not physically justify — the flare comes from a real source the camera swung past.
worlds.light_law: Available light only, and the stock cannot cope with it. Interiors: a window blows to pure white and the room around it crushes to black — there is no fill and no bounce, and the latitude is genuinely short. Fluorescent gives an uncorrected green cast; tungsten gives an uncorrected orange one; nobody balanced them. Exteriors: hard sun with blown skies, or flat overcast where everything is a middle grey. At night the image is barely exposed, grain swarms in the shadows, and a single street lamp or a car headlight is the only source there is.

palette_lock.bias: faded, uncorrected, grainy, warm. the window blows out and the shadow crushes and nobody corrected the cast. NO clean digital contrast, NO teal-orange grade, NO vintage-filter wash.

worlds.motion_cadence: 24fps with real motion blur and a shutter that was never quite right — motion carries a slight judder on fast pans. The camera corrects its own framing mid-shot. Frame-line weave and gate breathing are visible. Slow-motion does not exist here. Final hold 1.5s with the operator's stance still breathing and the grain still swarming.
negative_lock: NO clean digital sharpness, NO modern image with a film-grain overlay laid on top
Turkish label only — plain broadcast lower-third or a real sign in the scene, NO English signage
NO drone, NO crane, NO impossible vantage — the camera stands where a person could stand
NO staged reenactment posing, NO one looking into the lens on cue
NO colour-corrected perfection, NO matched white balance across sources
NO Instagram-vintage filter, NO fake light-leak sticker, NO added sepia wash
NO slow-motion, NO speed ramp
NO teal-orange Hollywood grade
NO named real people or recognisable public figures
worlds.example_injection: Photoreal archival frame, shoulder-held at head height on a period zoom, 16mm reversal grain swarming in the shadows. A shop shutter is being raised at dawn on an İstanbul street; the sky behind it blows to pure white and the shopfront crushes to black, halation blooming around the gap. The colour is uncorrected — slightly green from the shop's fluorescent, orange from the street lamp still on. A Turkish sign ('AÇIK') hangs real and slightly out of focus. Frame-line weave visible. Motion seed: the shutter rising, the operator re-framing late to follow it. AVOID: clean digital sharpness, grain overlay, drone vantage, staged pose, corrected colour, vintage filter. Clean motion-ready start frame.

worlds.name: Technical Cutaway — Exploded View & Mechanism
worlds.one_liner: Photoreal engineering cutaway: a real machine opened along a real plane, parts separated on their own assembly axes, every surface machined and worn — the mechanism explains itself by being visible.
worlds.render_law: Premium engineering cutaway and exploded-view visualisation — the craft lineage of high-end technical illustration and manufacturer sectioning, where a machine is opened so that its logic becomes visible without becoming a diagram. (1) THE CUT IS PHYSICAL — where the housing is sectioned, the cut face is a real machined plane: it has thickness, it shows the material's grain or grade through the wall, and its edge has a burr or a chamfer. It is never a soft dissolve, never a ghost, never a transparency effect. (2) THE EXPLOSION FOLLOWS THE ASSEMBLY — each part separates along the axis it is actually installed on: a bolt travels along its thread axis, a bearing along the shaft, a cover perpendicular to its face. The separation distances are proportional to the part's size, and the parts stay in their real orientation. A part flying off on an arbitrary vector destroys the whole illusion of an engineering document. (3) EVERY SURFACE HAS BEEN MADE — machining marks run in the direction the tool travelled, castings show their draft and their parting line, a stamped part has its own radius, threads have real pitch. Nothing is a smooth primitive. (4) WEAR IS INFORMATION — a contact surface is polished where it works, a gasket has taken a set, a fastener has witness marks. Wear says which parts touch. (5) THE MECHANISM IS SHOWN, NOT LABELLED — motion, load and clearance are read from the geometry; an arrow may not carry what the shape should. IMPERATIVE: PHOTOREAL ENGINEERING SECTION — real machined surfaces, real assembly axes, real material through the cut. Forbid ghost transparency, X-ray glow, arbitrary explosion vectors, arrow or callout standing in for a mechanism, smooth primitive shapes, and any part invented where the real one is unknown.
worlds.line_grammar: No outlines and no technical-drawing strokes. Edges read by the machining highlight along a chamfer, by the shadow inside a bore, and by the clean value break at the cut plane where the interior material meets the exterior finish. Hatching does not exist here — the cut face shows the MATERIAL, not a pattern.
worlds.lens_grammar: Technical primes: 85-135mm to keep the perspective near-orthographic so parallel edges stay parallel and the assembly reads as a document; 100mm macro for a bearing, a thread or a contact face. Deep stop (f/8-f/11) so the whole exploded stack stays sharp — a soft part at the back of an exploded view is a lie about what the viewer is allowed to inspect.
worlds.camera_grammar: Locked, near-orthographic technical coverage: a locked hero frame on the assembly axis, or a slow rotation about the mechanism's own centre of symmetry so the depth of the stack unfolds. A slow dolly along the assembly axis is allowed when the parts themselves define that axis. Never a whip pan, never a handheld shake, never a lens flare the frame does not physically justify.
worlds.light_law: Studio-technical and motivated: one large soft key placed high and slightly off-axis so every machined surface takes a directional sheen that reveals its tool marks; a second lower fill at two stops under, just enough to keep the inside of a bore readable rather than black. A hard grazing light skims across the cut face so the material's structure (grain, porosity, layers) shows in relief. Backgrounds fall to a controlled dark gradient so the assembly floats in readable space. No coloured light, no glow, no emission from anything that does not really emit.

palette_lock.bias: machined, metallic, neutral, precise. a directional sheen reveals tool marks and the bore stays readable. NO X-ray glow, NO neon tech wash, NO teal-orange.

worlds.motion_cadence: Real-time or slowed 24fps, and any slowing is uniform and honest. The explosion is a SEPARATION with real inertia: heavy parts start late and settle slowly, small parts move first. A mechanism in motion moves at its real ratio — a gear turns as many teeth as it meshes. Final hold 1.5s with the assembly at rest and one part still settling into place.
negative_lock: NO ghost transparency, NO X-ray glow, NO parts fading through each other
Turkish label only — plain technical lettering on a real part or plate, NO English signage
NO arbitrary explosion vector — every part separates along its real assembly axis
NO arrow, callout, chart or diagram overlay standing in for a mechanism
NO smooth primitive shape, NO untooled surface, NO part without a manufacturing history
NO part invented where the real one is unknown — leave it out of the section instead
NO neon tech wash, NO holographic UI, NO emission from a surface that does not emit
NO named real brands, models or manufacturer logos other than a locked client brand
NO teal-orange grade
worlds.example_injection: Photoreal engineering cutaway frame, 105mm near-orthographic, deep stop. A single-cylinder engine is sectioned along its bore axis: the cut face is a real machined plane with visible wall thickness and a chamfered burr, the casting's parting line running through it. The piston, rings and gudgeon pin are separated along the bore axis at distances proportional to their size, each still in its true orientation. A high soft key rakes across the machined faces and reveals the tool marks; the bore's interior stays readable rather than black. Wear polish is visible on the thrust face. A Turkish part plate ('ÜST KAPAK') is stamped into real metal. Motion seed: the piston beginning to settle back down the bore axis. AVOID: ghost transparency, X-ray glow, arrows, arbitrary explosion vector, smooth untooled surface. Clean motion-ready start frame.

worlds.name: Photoreal Anime Light — Hyper-Detailed Backgrounds
worlds.one_liner: Photoreal-background anime: a simple cel character standing inside a background painted with photographic density — lens flare, god-ray and bloom treated as real optics, and the sky doing the emotional work.
worlds.render_law: Contemporary photoreal-background anime — the Makoto Shinkai lineage, where a simply-drawn cel figure stands inside a background of near-photographic density and the WORLD carries the feeling the character does not state. (1) TWO REGISTERS IN ONE FRAME, AND THAT IS THE POINT — the character is clean 2D cel: flat two-value fill, thin uniform line, minimal interior detail. The background is the opposite: painted at photographic density with individual leaves, individual window reflections, individual raindrops, individual power lines. The gap between them is the style, not an error. (2) LIGHT IS THE SUBJECT — lens flare, anamorphic streak, bloom around every bright source, visible god-rays through dust and rain, chromatic fringing at high-contrast edges. These are OPTICAL events treated as real, painted into the plate rather than added over it. (3) THE SKY IS THE EMOTION — cumulus with sculptural undersides, contrails, a gradient running from deep blue at the zenith to warm gold at the horizon, weather doing the work a monologue would otherwise do. The sky is never empty and never generic. (4) MUNDANE MATTER, RENDERED PRECIOUS — a train carriage, a convenience-store shelf, a stairwell handrail, a puddle at a level crossing: ordinary things painted with such attention that they become the argument. (5) SPECULAR EVERYWHERE — every surface that could be wet, glassy or polished IS: rails, asphalt, window glass, tears, the rim of a cup. IMPERATIVE: CEL CHARACTER OVER PHOTOREAL PAINTED BACKGROUND — flat two-value character, photographic background density, optical light events painted in. Forbid a fully 3D CG character, a photoreal rendered human face, a flat or empty sky, painterly-soft backgrounds with no detail, and any lens flare added as a post overlay rather than painted into the plate.
worlds.line_grammar: Character: a thin uniform line, 1-2px, no taper, closing a flat two-value cel fill — the figure is deliberately simple. Background: NO line at all — form is built from painted value and detail density alone, exactly as a photograph builds it. The two grammars meet at the character's silhouette and the meeting is clean, not blended.
worlds.lens_grammar: Photographic optics painted into the plate: 24-35mm for a street, a platform or a room where the world must surround the figure; 50mm for the figure at natural distance; 85mm for a face with the background compressed into a field of bokeh discs. Bokeh is circular and has a defined edge. Chromatic fringing at high-contrast edges. Lens flare is anamorphic — a horizontal streak — and it comes from a source actually in frame.
worlds.camera_grammar: Composed, patient framing at human height: a locked frame with the figure small against the sky, a slow lateral drift that lets a foreground power line or platform edge pass as parallax, or a slow tilt from the ground up to the sky at the emotional beat. The camera treats the background as the co-lead — it takes time to look at it. Never a whip pan, never a handheld shake, never a lens flare the frame does not physically justify.
worlds.light_law: The sun is almost always low and almost always in frame or just outside it. Golden hour raking through a gap between buildings, breaking into anamorphic streak and bloom; sunlight through cloud gaps giving visible god-rays; every wet surface returning a hard specular. Interiors are lit by what is outside: a window, a passing train, a streetlight. Bloom haloes any bright source, and chromatic fringing sits at the high-contrast edges. Night is neon and vending-machine glow reflected in wet asphalt. There is no flat ambient anywhere.

palette_lock.bias: luminous, saturated, wistful, warm-cool. low sun breaks into streak and bloom and every wet surface returns it. NO flat ambient, NO desaturated wash, NO empty sky.

worlds.motion_cadence: Limited-animation character cadence (on twos for held beats) against a background that moves with photographic continuity — a train passing, a curtain lifting, rain falling at real speed. The emotional beat is a HOLD: the character still, the world moving around them. Bloom and flare shift as the source moves. Final hold 1.5-2s with the sky and the light continuing to change.
negative_lock: NO fully 3D CG character, NO photoreal rendered human face, NO uncanny skin
Turkish label only — hand-painted signage integrated into the background at photographic density, NO English signage
NO flat or empty sky — the sky is always doing something
NO soft painterly background without detail — the background is dense and specific
NO lens flare or bloom added as a post overlay — every optical event is painted into the plate
NO named franchise characters, locations or works
NO teal-orange grade, NO desaturated wash, NO flat ambient light
NO character rendered with the background's detail level — the gap between the two registers IS the style
worlds.example_injection: Photoreal-background anime frame, 35mm, low sun just outside the frame. A simply-drawn cel figure in a flat two-value uniform stands small on a level crossing in an İstanbul suburb; the background is painted at photographic density — individual power lines, individual leaves on a plane tree, individual raindrops on the rail, a Turkish crossing sign ('DUR') hand-painted with real weathering. Golden light breaks through the gap between two apartment blocks into an anamorphic streak, blooming, chromatic fringing on the wire above. The wet rail returns a hard specular. Cumulus with sculptural undersides fills the upper two-thirds. Motion seed: the barrier arm beginning to lift, the streak sliding across the frame as it does. AVOID: 3D character, photoreal face, empty sky, soft background, post-overlay flare, English signage. Clean motion-ready start frame.

worlds.name: Period Reconstruction — Photoreal Historical
worlds.one_liner: Photoreal historical reconstruction: a period lit only by what that period had — flame, oil, window — with material truth in cloth, stone and metal, and no modern trace anywhere in frame.
worlds.render_law: Premium historical reconstruction cinematography — the register of serious period filmmaking, where a past is rebuilt from its own materials and lit by its own sources, and credibility is the only currency. (1) THE PERIOD LIGHTS ITSELF — if the century had no electricity, the frame has none: a candle, an oil lamp, a hearth, a shaft of daylight through a high window, a torch in a corridor. The exposure is genuinely low and the falloff is genuinely steep — a face two metres from a candle is nearly dark, and that is the truth of the room. Modern soft-key comfort destroys the century instantly. (2) MATERIAL TRUTH IS THE ARGUMENT — hand-woven cloth with visible weave and honest dye irregularity; wool that has been worn and re-dyed; stone with tool marks from the chisel that cut it; iron that was forged, not milled, with hammer facets and real scale; wood with the saw's direction still in it. Nothing is machine-perfect, because nothing was. (3) DIRT IS HISTORY — hems are muddy, hands are stained by the work they do, walls are soot-blackened above every flame, and floors are worn where feet actually walked. A clean period is a costume party. (4) NO MODERN TRACE — no machine stitch, no zip, no plastic, no milled edge, no electric colour temperature, no straight power line on a horizon. One anachronism voids the frame. (5) SCALE IS HONEST — buildings are as big as they really were, and a room lit by one window is as dark as it really was. IMPERATIVE: PHOTOREAL PERIOD RECONSTRUCTION — period-only light sources, hand-made material truth, honest low exposure. Forbid modern soft-key lighting, machine-perfect cloth or metal, clean unworn surfaces, any anachronistic object or material, and any named real historical figure rendered recognisably.
worlds.line_grammar: No outlines. Edges are defined by flame-light falling on one side of a form and nothing on the other; a silhouette against a window is often the entire shape. Where the light is very low, edges dissolve honestly into the dark rather than being rescued by a rim.
worlds.lens_grammar: Period-drama primes: 35mm for a room and its architecture, 50mm for a figure at natural distance, 85mm for a face by a flame. Shot wide open (f/1.4-f/2) because the light genuinely demands it — the depth is shallow and that shallowness is a consequence, not a choice. Slight optical softness and honest falloff toward the corners; no clinical modern sharpness across the frame.
worlds.camera_grammar: Composed, deliberate period coverage: a locked frame at seated or standing eye height, a slow lateral dolly that lets a stone column pass as parallax, or a gentle handheld drift with the weight of a person carrying a camera in a real room. The camera does not float and does not fly. Never a whip pan, never a handheld shake used as style, never a lens flare the frame does not physically justify.
worlds.light_law: Only what the period had. Flame is the default: a candle, an oil lamp, a hearth, a torch — warm, low, moving, and falling off steeply with distance so a face at arm's length is lit and a face two paces back is nearly gone. Daylight enters through a real opening — a high window, a doorway, a gap in a roof — and reads as a hard shaft with dust in it, dying quickly into the room's depth. There is NO fill, NO bounce card, NO rim. If half the room is black, the room is half black, and the eye adjusts.

palette_lock.bias: flame-warm, earthen, soot-dark, low. light falls off steeply from the flame and the far wall is gone. NO modern soft key, NO cool fill, NO clean contrast.

worlds.motion_cadence: Real-time 24fps with true motion blur. Flame moves constantly and the light on every surface moves with it — nothing in a candle-lit frame is ever perfectly still. Cloth has weight and hangs. Dust hangs in a light shaft and turns slowly. Final hold 1.5s with the flame still moving and the shadows still breathing across the wall.
negative_lock: NO modern soft-key lighting, NO fill card, NO rim light — only period sources
Turkish label only — hand-carved, hand-painted or hand-written in a period letterform, NO English signage
NO anachronism: no machine stitch, no zip, no plastic, no milled edge, no electric colour temperature, no power line
NO machine-perfect cloth, metal or stone — everything was made by hand and shows it
NO clean unworn surface, NO costume-party freshness
NO named real historical figure rendered recognisably
NO teal-orange grade, NO desaturated 'historical' wash, NO sepia filter
NO drone or crane vantage
worlds.example_injection: Photoreal period reconstruction frame, 85mm wide open, lit only by a single oil lamp on the table. A pair of hands works a hand-woven cloth in a stone room; the lamp is the whole light plan and its falloff is steep — the hands and the cloth are lit, the far wall is gone. Soot blackens the stone above the flame. The weave is irregular, the dye uneven, the wool worn and re-dyed. Chisel marks run across the stone sill. A Turkish word ('SABIR') is carved into the wooden edge of the table in a period letterform. Dust hangs in the lamp's light and turns. Motion seed: the flame leaning as air moves, every shadow in the room leaning with it. AVOID: modern soft key, fill light, machine-perfect cloth, clean surface, anachronism, sepia wash. Clean motion-ready start frame.

materials.name: Materyal Yok — World Native
materials.substance_grammar: No teaching-material substance layer. World renders natively as described in its render_law. Use for cinematic real worlds, high-end brand work, and any prompt where the subject is the world's native form (character, object, environment).

materials.name: Paper Craft Pop-Up
materials.substance_grammar: Teaching subject is constructed of die-cut cardstock (100-250 gsm equivalent visible fiber): tabs, folds, hinge mechanisms, layered pop-up. Paper grain visible at raking light, fiber tear at cut edges, warm ambient fold-shadows. INSIDE the selected world's render_law — world dictates HOW paper reads (Pixar 3D shading paper vs. Ghibli watercolor-painting paper); paper does NOT replace world. Use for concrete manipulable teaching props (math tiles, letter cards, story pages, geometry shapes).

materials.name: Clay / Hamur
materials.substance_grammar: Teaching subject is stop-motion plasticine clay: matt tone, visible fingerprint or tool marks, slight deformation between frames suggesting hand-manipulation. Character skin stays true to world's render_law (Pixar 3D skin is Pixar skin, NOT clay). Only PROPS are clay: tiles, arrows, animals, letters, numbers. Fingerprints on clay are storytelling detail. Use for tactile counting, shape manipulation, playful transformation demos.

materials.name: Chalkboard / Kara Tahta
materials.substance_grammar: Teaching happens on a black or dark-green chalkboard surface as backdrop, with real physical props (numbered wooden blocks, colored pencils, math manipulatives) sitting on the chalkboard tray or in front. Chalk drawings on the board are hand-drawn in the world's line grammar — Pixar 3D world gives clean beveled chalk, Deakins world gives naturalist chalk with real chalk-dust particulate. Use for classroom-authentic scenes.

materials.name: Wood Toy — Tactile
materials.substance_grammar: Teaching props are hand-turned wooden toys (Montessori-style): satin-varnished maple or oak, visible grain, anisotropic highlight from oblique light, real shadow. Numbers, letters, geometric solids, sorting shapes all in polished wood. INSIDE the world — Pixar 3D world renders wood with feature-animation subsurface fidelity; Deakins world renders wood photoreal with natural light rake. Use for warm, natural, Montessori-aligned teaching.

materials.name: Storybook Illustration
materials.substance_grammar: Teaching subject appears as a classic children's book watercolor-and-ink illustration nested within the world. If world is Ghibli, the storybook IS the world; if world is Pixar 3D, an illustrated storybook page opens within the 3D scene like a physical prop. Watercolor pigment, paper grain, torn edges, hand lettering. Colors muted, edges soft. Use for hikaye-anlatım (storytelling) sequences and fable-style teaching.

materials.name: Notebook Ink
materials.substance_grammar: Teaching happens on a lined notebook page, spiral-bound, with pen-and-ink drawings appearing as if hand-drawn in real-time by an unseen author. Ink bleed at nib pressure, slight paper warp under wet ink, occasional smudge. Ink lines follow the world's line grammar — Arcane world gives brushy varied-weight ink; JJK/MAPPA world gives confident 2px black ink; Deakins world gives sparse graphite. Use for editorial/documentary teaching, brand storytelling, older-grade abstract concepts.

materials.name: One Piece — Toei Bold-Cel (IP Style)
materials.substance_grammar: IP drawing-style clause, not a Render World: applies a Toei bold-cel grammar on top of whichever world/subject is selected — 3-5px uniform-weight pure black outline, silhouette-first minimal interior line, flat 2-value cel fill in poster-vibrant saturated color, rubber-elastic anatomy stretch/squash at peak action (joints elongate, eyes widen in reaction, mouth can exceed face silhouette in a shout). Combine with any Render World; the World still governs environment and lighting quality, this clause only governs how the subject's line and fill read. Never depict Luffy, Zoro, Nami, Sanji, Chopper, any named One Piece character, the Straw Hat emblem, Jolly Roger flags, or named franchise locations — original subjects only, rendered in this grammar.

materials.name: Naruto — Pierrot Chakra-Cel (IP Style)
materials.substance_grammar: IP drawing-style clause, not a Render World: applies a Pierrot shinobi-cel grammar on top of whichever world/subject is selected — clean confident 2-3px black line, warm golden-orange determination palette bias, chakra energy visualized as one bright spiraling arc or circular trace around the subject (never a named jutsu hand-sign), warm dust-trail motion accents. Combine with any Render World. Never depict Naruto, Sasuke, Sakura, Kakashi, the Hidden Leaf headband symbol, Akatsuki cloak, or named franchise locations — original subjects only, rendered in this grammar.

materials.name: Demon Slayer — Ufotable Elemental (IP Style)
materials.substance_grammar: IP drawing-style clause, not a Render World: applies an ufotable elemental-composite grammar on top of whichever world/subject is selected — tight confident linework composited with painterly 3D elemental FX, one breathing-pattern ribbon arc of light/water/fire trailing the subject's movement, dense particle bloom at impact. Combine with any Render World. Never depict Tanjiro, Nezuko, Zenitsu, Inosuke, named breathing-technique forms, or named franchise locations — original subjects only, rendered in this grammar.

materials.name: Solo Leveling — MAPPA Manhwa (IP Style)
materials.substance_grammar: IP drawing-style clause, not a Render World: applies a MAPPA manhwa power-ascent grammar on top of whichever world/subject is selected — razor-sharp angular linework, high-fidelity rendering density, cold dark-blue/steel-grey dominant palette, power-aura visualized as uplifting ground particles and a faint violet-indigo silhouette outline at climax. Combine with any Render World. Never depict Sung Jin-Woo, shadow-soldier designs, the monarch aura signature, or named franchise locations — original subjects only, rendered in this grammar.

materials.name: Arcane — Fortiche Painterly (IP Style)
materials.substance_grammar: IP drawing-style clause, not a Render World: applies a Fortiche painterly-3D grammar on top of whichever world/subject is selected — visible oil-paint brush-stroke albedo texture on every surface plane over a 3D form, graphic hard-edged shadow massing (not soft gradient falloff), desaturated moody base with exactly one saturated accent. Combine with any Render World. Never depict Jinx, Vi, Caitlyn, Piltover/Zaun iconography, or named franchise locations — original subjects only, rendered in this grammar.

materials.name: Jujutsu Kaisen — MAPPA Cursed Ink (IP Style)
materials.substance_grammar: IP drawing-style clause, not a Render World (distinct from the jjk_mappa Render World — this is the portable drawing-grammar version, usable inside any other world): confident 2-3px black line at rest dissolving into a 1-2 frame abstract ink-smear brush streak at peak action, cold underexposed shadow bias, abstract black fractal energy tendrils with a single cursed-teal core accent (never a named curse or technique). Combine with any Render World. Never depict Yuji, Gojo, Sukuna, the domain-expansion visual signature, or named franchise locations — original subjects only, rendered in this grammar.

materials.name: Dragon Ball — Toriyama Bold (IP Style)
materials.substance_grammar: IP drawing-style clause, not a Render World: applies a Toriyama bold-outline grammar on top of whichever world/subject is selected — thick uniform black outline, muscular simplified anatomy silhouette, primary-color impact palette (orange/blue/gold), power-aura visualized as a hard-rim energy burst radiating from the subject with open-field staging. Combine with any Render World. Never depict Goku, Vegeta, named power-pose signatures, or named franchise locations — original subjects only, rendered in this grammar.

materials.name: Attack on Titan — Scale Dread (IP Style)
materials.substance_grammar: IP drawing-style clause, not a Render World: applies a WIT/MAPPA desaturated-scale grammar on top of whichever world/subject is selected — grey-green military desaturation, near-monochrome except one muted signal color, colossal-scale composition bias (subject dwarfed by environment or the reverse), harsh survival practical lighting with no warm bounce. Combine with any Render World. Never depict Eren, Mikasa, Levi, the Titan-transformation signature, the Survey Corps insignia, or named franchise locations — original subjects only, rendered in this grammar.

materials.name: Watercolor
materials.substance_grammar: Teaching subject/prop is rendered as academic watercolor paint on cold-press paper: wet-on-wet bleeding edges where colors diffuse into each other, visible granulation texture pooling in wash fields, untouched white paper left as the brightest highlight (never opaque white paint). Applies INSIDE the selected world's render_law — world dictates subject, this materializes it as watercolor medium. Use for soft nature/science/wonder teaching content, seasons, biology.

materials.name: Wax Crayon
materials.substance_grammar: Teaching subject/prop is rendered as bold wax-crayon strokes: visible waxy resist texture where crayon skips across paper tooth, thick primary-color strokes with child-honest hand-pressure variation, colors mix by physical layering where strokes overlap (never a digital blend). Applies INSIDE the selected world's render_law. Use for playful, bold, early-grade lesson starters and art-education content.

materials.name: Ink Brush
materials.substance_grammar: Teaching subject/prop is rendered as East Asian sumi ink-brush painting on rice paper: calligraphic stroke weight (thick-to-thin within one gesture), visible brush-hair separation at stroke ends, generous white breathing-space negative space around the subject, ink-wash gradation where water dilutes the black. Applies INSIDE the selected world's render_law. Use for editorial, philosophical, or procedural teaching content that benefits from restraint and negative space.

materials.name: Neon Tube
materials.substance_grammar: Teaching subject/prop is rendered as if built from curved neon glass tubes: colored light glowing from within the glass itself (not surface-lit), soft halo bloom on any dark surrounding surface, visible tube-bend geometry at every corner, faint buzz-flicker implied by uneven glow intensity. Applies INSIDE the selected world's render_law. Use for night-atmosphere, urban, or energetic modern-concept teaching content.

refs.name: Analog Neon Impact Timing
refs.use: Grammar only, original subjects only — layers Otomo's analog-bloom neon-night and hard-edged impact timing onto the chosen world; use when a night-city or power-surge beat needs 80s film-cel weight without any franchise identity.
refs.avoid: NO named franchise characters, vehicles, props, or locations; original subjects only. NO biomechanical body-horror mutation signature. NO modern digital-clean neon glow (bloom must read analog-optical). NO 3D render.
refs.dna: Stylized animation frame in the 1988 Otomo theatrical-cel lineage (Tokyo Movie Shinsha film-grade animation). Medium/era: late-80s 24fps theatrical cel painted to film finish, analog optical compositing. Named anchor: Otomo light-bloom impact and dense-neon night. Signature light: hard practical neon and sodium sources bloom with analog optical-printer halation rings; a single hot source rim-lights the subject against crushing black; a power-surge event throws a brief hard-edged light-blast that reads as pure white core before the color. Color/grade: deep blue-black night dominant, hot magenta and electric-red neon accents, sodium-amber street pools — saturated where lit, near-black everywhere else. Lens/optics: flat 2D, simulated wide 24-35mm for street scale, telephoto compression on the hero; no DOF, depth by atmospheric haze and neon density. Texture/render: confident thin cel line at obsessive interior density (machine detail, cable, rubble), painted-to-film cel with visible airbrush gradients on metal and blast-light. Composition+motion: held tension then a single-frame hard-edged smear on impact, debris arcing on real ballistic paths, camera locked while the destruction moves; the light-blast holds one frame then decays.
refs.anchor: Otomo neon-impact: analog optical-bloom neon over crushing black, single-frame hard smear on impact, painted-to-film cel with airbrushed blast-light, ballistic debris, locked camera.

refs.name: Anime Silhouette Discipline
refs.use: Grammar only, original subjects only — layers an anime silhouette-readability discipline onto the chosen world; use when a beat must read instantly and cleanly, staging every pose as a legible silhouette.
refs.avoid: NO named anime character or franchise design. NO cluttered pose with props overlapping the body-line. NO flat frontal lighting that kills separation, NO ambiguous silhouette, NO 3D render.
refs.dna: Stylized animation story-DNA frame in an anime silhouette-discipline lineage (cel-grammar readability doctrine, original subjects only). Medium/era: general anime cel-animation staging as a readability discipline. Named anchor: silhouette-first discipline — every pose, prop, and character readable as a pure black silhouette. Signature light: strong separation light — a rim or backlight that carves the subject's silhouette cleanly against the ground; contre-jour and rim-separation favored so the shape reads instantly. Color/grade: whatever the world's grade, but staged for maximum silhouette contrast — subject-against-ground separation prioritized. Lens/optics: flat 2D, composed for silhouette clarity — the pose profiled or angled so the read is unambiguous, props held clear of the body-line; no DOF. Texture/render: confident cel line, but the discipline is compositional — poses designed so the silhouette alone communicates the action, hands and props clear of overlap. Composition+motion: silhouette-first staging — a held pose that reads as a clean black shape, action clear in profile, the negative space around the body kept legible; the readability of the silhouette is the craft, motion staged to keep every frame's shape clean.
refs.anchor: Anime silhouette discipline: silhouette-first pose readability, rim/contre-jour separation carving the subject from ground, props clear of the body-line, action legible as a pure black shape, clean negative space.

refs.name: Apple Object Worship
refs.use: Grammar only, original subjects only — layers a premium object-worship reverent product-lighting and seamless-sweep grammar onto the chosen world; use for a pristine, premium, hero-object reveal beat.
refs.avoid: NO real brand logos or identifiable branded-product designs, NO real brand identity. NO cluttered background, NO harsh uncontrolled reflection, NO saturated grade, NO grunge, NO neon.
refs.dna: Photoreal product frame in the Apple object-worship lineage (reverent hero-product grammar, original subjects only). Medium/era: contemporary premium product cinematography. Named anchor: object worship — the reverent hero product, seamless gradient sweep, soft controlled studio light. Signature light: large soft studio sources shaping a controlled gradient falloff across the product surface; a clean edge-defining rim; the light is immaculate, reverent, worshipful — every reflection intentional. Color/grade: clean minimal palette — a seamless gradient background (soft grey-to-white or a single deep tone), the product surface pristine; restrained, premium, exact. Lens/optics: 85-100mm macro with a razor-thin focus plane, or a clean 50mm hero; controlled programmed moves; pristine clarity, no distortion. Texture/render: flawless surface rendering — brushed metal, glass, matte composite with correct roughness; every reflection and edge-highlight placed with reverence; immaculate studio finish. Composition+motion: the product centered and worshipped in clean negative space, a seamless gradient sweep behind, a slow reverent hero-reveal rotation or push; the immaculate light and pristine surface carry the object-worship.
refs.anchor: Apple object worship: reverent hero product in clean negative space, large soft studio sources with controlled gradient falloff, seamless gradient sweep background, immaculate placed reflections, slow reverent reveal.

refs.name: Painterly Clay Hybrid
refs.use: Grammar only, original subjects only — layers a Fortiche-painterly grammar with a clay-tactile surface warmth onto the chosen world; use for a premium-stylized beat that wants both painterly edge and handmade tactility.
refs.avoid: NO named franchise characters, NO iconic costumes or emblems, NO named powers, techniques, or signature moves, NO recognizable franchise locations; original subjects only.
refs.dna: Stylized animation frame in a Fortiche-painterly-meets-clay-tactility hybrid lineage (painted-3D grammar over sculpt-tactile surface, original subjects only). Medium/era: Fortiche impasto 2D-over-3D fused with a clay-sculpt tactility. Named anchor: painterly-clay hybrid — oil-paint brush stroke at every silhouette edge with a hand-sculpted clay surface warmth. Signature light: motivated single-direction key with a complementary-hue bounce (teal key → magenta fill), a jewel-tone accent rim, painterly-soft warm AO (no hard black); the clay surface catches a soft tactile sheen. Color/grade: Fortiche-derived warm-highlight/cool-shadow curve, deep-violet darkest shadow (no pure black), magenta-and-teal two-color rim system, with an earthy clay-warm undertone. Lens/optics: 35-50mm, f/2.8-f/4, painterly halation on hot sources, 35mm grain in every value step. Texture/render: visible brush stroke at every silhouette edge over a surface that reads sculpted-clay — hand-painted blush and a soft clay-matte tactility, hair as painted-sculpted clumps, fingerprint-soft form imperfection. Composition+motion: deliberate 24fps with painted-clay smear frames at peak, slow-in/slow-out weight, a 1.5-2s emotional hold; the painted-clay surface warmth invites touch.
refs.anchor: Painterly-clay hybrid: oil-paint silhouette stroke over sculpted-clay-matte surface, deep-violet shadow (no black), teal-magenta bounce system, painterly-soft warm AO, clay-tactile form imperfection, painted smear frames.

refs.name: Fortiche Painted Texture Grammar

refs.name: Kon Reality-Fold Match Cut
refs.use: Applies Kon's editorial shape-fold grammar — shape-match on cut, waking overlap, aspect-echo, hard cut on gesture — as a transition and editing logic layer over the chosen world.
refs.avoid: NO named franchise films, scenes, characters, or poster/TV imagery; original subjects only. NO psychological-horror franchise imagery. NO repeating-clone crowd (must be individual behavior). NO smooth glossy modern anime sheen.
refs.dna: Stylized animation editing in the Satoshi Kon shape-fold grammar (Perfect Blue, Millennium Actress, Paprika, Paranoia Agent, Tokyo Godfathers). Medium/era: 1997–2006 Madhouse theatrical/OVA animation, precise editorial animation. Named anchor: Kon shape-match cut — the final shape in frame A becomes the opening shape in frame B; the cut IS the storytelling. Signature light: no dominant light style — Kon's lighting serves the match-cut logic; scene-fold transitions must carry consistent value-structure across the cut so the eye reads the shape-echo before registering the scene change; interior fluorescent, exterior overcast, memory-golden are the three operating light registers depending on layer (reality / memory / dream). Color/grade: high-fidelity Tokyo urban palette for reality-layer (cool neon-tinged night, fluorescent interior flat, concrete grey overcast); memory-layer shifts to warm sepia-amber; dream-layer is hyper-saturated impossible-color; the shift in grade IS the layer signal. Lens/optics: simulated 35–50mm; depth of field present and used to separate layers (sharp in current layer, soft-dreamy in memory/dream layer); NO handheld; camera moves are authored and purposeful, never observational. Texture/render: Madhouse-quality cel linework; crowd animation is dense and observationally specific (individual behavior, not repeating crowds); urban backgrounds are architecturally specific (Tokyo detail) but originals only. Composition+motion: aspect-echo compositions — the geometric proportion of subject-to-negative-space in frame A must mirror frame B; hard cut on gesture peak (hand raise, door open, object thrown) where the gesture continues in the new scene; screen-direction carries across the cut; waking overlap: the final 4–6 frames of a dream sequence overlay with the opening of the waking scene.
refs.anchor: Satoshi Kon shape-match cut: frame A's closing shape becomes frame B's opening shape, hard cut on gesture peak, waking overlap 4–6 frames, dense observational crowd, aspect-echo compositions across the cut.
