Signature light: the practical the worker actually works under — a task lamp, a machine glow, a window at the bench — as key; the face lit by what the WORK gives off, not by a beauty light. Color/grade: honest skin, industrial neutrals, one warm value coming from the work itself (a hot metal, a lit screen, a sodium fixture). Lens/optics: 50mm for presence at human distance, 85mm to isolate the hands; shallow enough to separate, never so shallow the workplace disappears. Texture/render: real pore, real grime under a nail, worn fabric at the elbow, a scratched machine face — wear is credibility. Composition+motion: hands occupy the lower third with the tool's line leading out of frame; the half-second is the completion of one small action — the gauge reaching its mark, the part seating home.

refs.name: Scale Without Spectacle
refs.anchor: restrained scale grammar: the size of the operation read through a person standing inside it, held wide and still, no swelling move
refs.use: Grammar only — use for the scale beat (a plant floor, a hangar, a server hall) when the world must feel large without the frame shouting.
refs.avoid: NO drone-epic skyline cliché; NO swelling crane reveal; NO oversaturated ad gloss; NO lens flare; NO empty spectacle without a human for scale.
refs.dna: Photoreal corporate brand-film still in the restrained-scale institutional lineage. Medium/era: contemporary prestige commercial, wide prime, locked or barely moving. Named anchor: scale is proved by a person, not by a wide lens. Signature light: the space's own overhead fixtures give an even ambient base; one raking source (a bay door, a skylight, a row of windows) crosses the depth and gives the volume its shape. Color/grade: cool industrial neutrals with a single warm human note; deep readable shadow, never crushed to nothing. Lens/optics: 24–35mm at a deep stop, camera at chest height and level — no low-angle heroism, no high-angle diminishment. Texture/render: honest surfaces at real scale — the floor has coatings and repairs, the ceiling has services, the machines have decades on them. Composition+motion: one human placed small and deliberately off-centre, the architecture's repetition running past them to a vanishing point. The half-second is stillness with one working element continuing: a line advancing, a fan turning, steam releasing.

refs.name: Crowd as Texture
refs.anchor: civic-crowd grammar: a gathering read as a field of individual people, each one legible, compressed by a long lens into a texture of faces and shoulders
refs.use: Grammar only — use when the beat is MANY: a square, a march, a queue, a gathering. Keeps the crowd human instead of turning it into a mass.
refs.avoid: NO uniformed formation unless the cast names it; NO copy-paste faces; NO named real people or public figures; NO propaganda triumphalism; NO flag as a graphic overlay.
refs.dna: Photoreal civic broadcast still in the public-ceremony documentary lineage. Medium/era: contemporary broadcast commercial, 85–135mm compression from a fixed vantage. Named anchor: a crowd is a lot of individuals, and the lens must let each be one. Signature light: real directional sun — low and raking so each head casts its own shadow into the next, or flat overcast where the light is honest and nobody is favoured; no supplementary fill. Color/grade: true skin across a wide range of tones, ordinary clothing colours left ordinary; the accent, if any, is a single fabric moving in the wind. Lens/optics: 85–135mm at f/4, compressing depth so ranks of people stack into a legible texture; the front row is sharp and the fifth row is still a person. Texture/render: real fabric weave, real hair in real wind, real breath in cold air; nobody retouched. Composition+motion: the crowd fills the frame edge to edge with no empty ceremonial space; the half-second is a ripple passing through them — a turn of heads, a lifted hand travelling down the rank.

refs.name: Cloth & Wind Physics
refs.anchor: cloth-in-wind grammar: fabric carries the emotion — a flag, a headscarf, a curtain read as weight and cloth, never as a graphic
refs.use: Grammar only — use when the beat needs the air to be visible. Gives the frame something honest to move without inventing an event.
refs.avoid: NO flag as flat graphic or overlay; NO CGI cloth simulation look; NO wind machine perfection; NO slow-motion heroism; NO named real people.
refs.dna: Photoreal civic broadcast still in the cloth-and-wind documentary lineage. Medium/era: contemporary broadcast commercial, 85–135mm, natural wind only. Named anchor: cloth is a body with weight, not a texture on a plane. Signature light: raking sun across the fabric so the folds read as depth and the weave shows; light passing THROUGH the cloth where it thins, giving one translucent value the solid areas do not have. Color/grade: the cloth's own colour at honest saturation; the surround held back so the fabric is the loudest surface. Lens/optics: 85–135mm at f/4 — long enough to compress the cloth against its background and pull it forward as a plane in space. Texture/render: real weave, real hem, real fray; the fabric has been outdoors and shows it. Composition+motion: the cloth occupies one third of the frame and its motion crosses the dominant element without covering it; the half-second is ONE gust arriving — the fold beginning to lift, the hem not yet answering.

refs.name: Monument at Human Height
refs.anchor: civic-monument grammar: stone and inscription photographed at the height of the person standing before it, never from below in triumph
refs.use: Grammar only — use for the memory or the meaning beat: a stone, a plaque, a threshold. Keeps the civic object honest and human-scaled.
refs.avoid: NO low-angle triumphalism; NO named real monuments, people or events rendered recognisably; NO golden-hour propaganda glow; NO floating overlay text.
refs.dna: Photoreal civic broadcast still in the human-height memorial lineage (invented, generic monuments only). Medium/era: contemporary broadcast commercial, 50mm at eye level. Named anchor: a monument is met, not worshipped — the camera stands where a person stands. Signature light: honest daylight raking across carved stone so the incision reads by shadow alone; overcast is equally valid and often truer; no glow, no halo, no rim added for reverence. Color/grade: stone greys and earth tones held true, one warm value if the sun is genuinely low; never a golden wash laid over the whole frame. Lens/optics: 50mm at chest-to-eye height, level; the stone is as tall as it really is and no taller. Texture/render: real carve depth, real weathering, real lichen and rain-streak in the letterforms; hands that touch it leave real contact. Composition+motion: the stone and the person share the frame at comparable weight, neither dominating; the half-second is a hand arriving at the surface, or a shadow moving one degree across a carved line.

refs.name: Steam & Heat Physics
refs.anchor: appetite-heat grammar: steam backlit into a visible column, the dish read as HOT — condensation on the rim, a surface still releasing
refs.use: Grammar only — use when the dish must be understood as just-cooked. Heat is the appetite signal; the frame must prove it, not claim it.
refs.avoid: NO CGI steam plume; NO dry-ice fog; NO glycerine sheen standing in for real moisture; NO named real brands other than a locked client brand; NO cold food pretending to be hot.
refs.dna: Photoreal tabletop food-hero still in the appetite-appeal culinary advertising lineage. Medium/era: contemporary food studio, 100mm macro, dish shot within seconds of the heat. Named anchor: heat is a light problem before it is a food problem. Signature light: one hard backlight or 3/4 rear key at low elevation catching the steam column so it separates from a dark background; the near side held by a single white bounce so the dish itself never falls into silhouette. Color/grade: the food's own colour at full honest saturation — a sear is brown, a herb is green, an oil is amber; the surround stays muted so nothing competes with the plate. Lens/optics: 100mm macro at f/4–f/5.6; the focal plane sits on the steam's origin, not on the rim. Texture/render: real gloss where fat has rendered, real char where flame touched, real condensation with real contact angles on the rim; every texture is a cooking event, not a coating. Composition+motion: the dish sits low in frame with the steam column rising into negative space; the half-second is a release — steam curling, a bead of moisture beginning to run, a crust settling.

refs.name: Cut & Tear Reveal
refs.anchor: appetite-reveal grammar: the interior is the promise — a cut, a tear, a pull that opens the food and shows what was hidden
refs.use: Grammar only — use for the payoff beat. The inside of the food is the argument; the frame exists to open it.
refs.avoid: NO fake-perfect cross-section; NO glue, foam or non-food propping visible; NO named real brands other than a locked client brand; NO bite that leaves no evidence.
refs.dna: Photoreal tabletop food-hero still in the culinary reveal advertising lineage. Medium/era: contemporary food studio, 100mm macro, real food cut on camera. Named anchor: the cut is the sentence — everything before it was setup. Signature light: a low raking key across the cut face so the interior structure (crumb, grain, layer, melt) reads by its own micro-shadow; a second soft source lifts the cut plane just enough that it does not go black. Color/grade: the interior carries the highest saturation in frame — a molten centre, a pink grain, a bright crumb — and the exterior stays a step back. Lens/optics: 100mm macro at f/5.6, focal plane locked on the cut face and nowhere else. Texture/render: real crumb, real melt with real viscosity, real fibre when it tears; the blade or the hand deforms the food as a real tool would. Composition+motion: the cut runs across the frame's short axis so the reveal is read left-to-right; the half-second is the SEPARATION — the two halves parting, the pull just begun, gravity not yet having its say.

refs.name: Pour & Viscosity Truth
refs.anchor: appetite-pour grammar: a sauce, honey or drink pouring at ITS OWN speed — viscosity is character and the frame must respect it
refs.use: Grammar only — use when the beat is a pour. Thick things move slowly and thin things move fast; getting that wrong destroys the appetite before the colour does.
refs.avoid: NO gravity-free pour; NO CGI gel; NO speed-ramped fake slow-motion; NO named real brands other than a locked client brand; NO pour that never lands.
refs.dna: Photoreal tabletop food-hero still in the pour-and-viscosity advertising lineage. Medium/era: contemporary food studio, 100mm macro, high-speed honest capture. Named anchor: viscosity is the character of the thing — a syrup is not a juice moving slowly. Signature light: backlight through the pouring stream so it glows along its length and the thickness of the column is visible; the receiving surface catches a real specular where the stream lands. Color/grade: the poured material owns the accent and the highest saturation; the vessel and surround stay near-neutral. Lens/optics: 100mm macro at f/5.6, focal plane at the LANDING point, not at the lip. Texture/render: real surface tension — a thick pour ropes and coils, a thin pour breaks into droplets; the receiving surface deforms, absorbs or beads according to what it actually is. Composition+motion: the stream enters from an upper frame edge and lands on the hero in the lower third; the half-second is the LANDING — the crater forming, the first coil settling, the ripple beginning to travel.

refs.name: Sweat & Breath Truth
refs.anchor: gym-truth grammar: effort proved by the body — sweat with weight, breath with volume, skin flushed by real work
refs.use: Grammar only — use for the effort beat. Effort is a physiological fact and the frame must photograph the fact, not the pose.
refs.avoid: NO oiled fitness-model gloss; NO plastic AI skin; NO posing that stops the work; NO named real athletes; NO oversaturated ad grade.
refs.dna: Photoreal sport commercial still in the gym-truth advertising lineage. Medium/era: contemporary sport commercial, 85–100mm, handheld at the edge of the effort. Named anchor: sweat has weight and breath has volume — photograph both. Signature light: the room's own hard overhead source gives a top key that finds the sweat as specular; one window shaft or ring practical rakes across from the side and carves the working muscle. Color/grade: skin flushed and honest across all tones, the gym's cool fluorescent base against the warm blood in the face; no orange tan push, no crushed contrast heroism. Lens/optics: 85–100mm at f/2.8 compressing the face or the fist, the background collapsing to unreadable value so nothing distracts from the strain. Texture/render: real pore, real sweat bead with real contact angle, real chalk dust, real tape fraying at a knuckle; hair wet where it should be wet. Composition+motion: the body enters the frame under load and the frame does not have room for it — the crop is tight enough to feel confined. The half-second is the point of maximum strain: the bar bending, the breath held, nothing yet released.

refs.name: Impact Compression
refs.anchor: impact grammar: the instant of contact — flesh, glove or ball deforming, the whole frame compressed into one collision plane
refs.use: Grammar only — use for the collision beat. The frame's job is to prove that two things really met.
refs.avoid: NO speed-ramped fake slow-motion; NO CGI shockwave; NO named real athletes; NO impact without deformation; NO crowd reaction pasted behind.
refs.dna: Photoreal sport commercial still in the contact-instant advertising lineage. Medium/era: contemporary sport commercial, 100mm, high shutter, honest capture. Named anchor: contact is a deformation, not a flash. Signature light: a hard practical or ring source directly behind and above the contact point so both surfaces get a specular edge at the instant they meet; the room stays two stops down so the collision is the only lit event. Color/grade: high honest contrast — the room dark, the contact plane bright; one saturated accent (a glove, a lane line, a jersey) and nothing else. Lens/optics: 100mm at f/4, focal plane locked on the contact point; a millimetre either way and the frame is a lie. Texture/render: real deformation — glove leather creases, flesh gives, the ball flattens, chalk and sweat leave the surface in a real spray with real trajectories. Composition+motion: the contact point sits on a third, the two bodies entering from opposing frame edges so the collision has direction. The half-second is the instant of maximum deformation — nothing has rebounded yet.

refs.name: Ring-Light Isolation
refs.anchor: arena-isolation grammar: one hard overhead source, everything outside its cone falling to black — the athlete alone in a lit island
refs.use: Grammar only — use for the solitude or resolve beat. The dark is the opponent; the light is the work.
refs.avoid: NO haze machine theatrics; NO god-ray beam the fixture cannot justify; NO named real athletes or venues; NO crowd invented in the dark; NO teal-orange grade.
refs.dna: Photoreal sport commercial still in the overhead-isolation advertising lineage. Medium/era: contemporary sport commercial, 50–85mm, locked or slow push. Named anchor: one lamp, one body, and everything the lamp does not reach. Signature light: a single hard overhead source directly above the subject, its cone falling off fast so the floor is a bright island in a dark room; the face is lit from above and the eye sockets take honest shadow — this is not a flattering light and it should not be. Color/grade: deep true black outside the cone, no lift; skin held honest inside it; a single warm value in the fixture's own colour. Lens/optics: 50mm for the body in its island, 85mm to isolate the face; the stop deep enough that the edge of the light cone stays legible. Texture/render: dust and moisture in the air rendered only where the cone reaches them; canvas, mat or floor showing its real wear inside the light. Composition+motion: the subject stands at the cone's centre with dead space above; the half-second is the smallest possible motion — a breath, a shift of weight, a head lifting one degree.

refs.name: Window-Light Study
refs.anchor: aspirational-study grammar: window daylight as the whole light plan, a person working at the plane the light already falls on
refs.use: Grammar only — use for the study or focus beat. The light does the persuading; nothing is added to sell it.
refs.avoid: NO stock-photo smiling-at-camera; NO plastic AI skin; NO fake bokeh; NO staged pointing at a screen; NO named real institutions or logos.
refs.dna: Photoreal education commercial still in the aspirational institutional lineage. Medium/era: contemporary premium promo, full-frame digital, 50–85mm. Named anchor: the desk is by the window because that is where the work happens. Signature light: window daylight as the single soft key, falling across the working plane and dying honestly into the room; visible overhead fixtures allowed as background value only. Color/grade: daylight-neutral, skin honest across tones, paper white but not blown; one institutional accent colour appearing where the room genuinely carries it. Lens/optics: 50mm for the person in their room, 85mm at f/2.8 for the hands and the page; shallow enough to isolate, never so shallow the place disappears. Texture/render: real paper grain, real pencil deposit, real screen glow on skin, real dust in the window light; the desk has been used and shows it. Composition+motion: the person occupies the lit third with the window as an unseen source off-frame; the half-second is a small honest act — a page turning, a line being written, eyes moving down.

refs.name: Hands-On Material
refs.anchor: learning-by-doing grammar: the subject learned through a real object in real hands — the material carries the lesson, not a slide
refs.use: Grammar only — use when the beat is UNDERSTANDING. Show the thing being handled, and the concept arrives with it.
refs.avoid: NO floating UI overlay; NO holographic diagram; NO icon standing in for the object; NO plastic AI skin; NO named real institutions or logos.
refs.dna: Photoreal education commercial still in the hands-on institutional lineage. Medium/era: contemporary premium promo, 50–100mm, table-height. Named anchor: the concept has a body, and the hands are holding it. Signature light: a soft key from the room's real window or panel falling across the object; the hands lit by the same source so nothing about the moment is staged. Color/grade: honest skin, honest material colour; the object's own hue is the accent and the room stays a step back. Lens/optics: 85–100mm at f/4, focal plane on the point of contact between hand and object; the face may be present but is never sharper than the work. Texture/render: real material — wood grain, circuit solder, clay, glass, metal — with real fingerprints and real handling wear; the object has been used to learn with. Composition+motion: hands and object hold the lower two thirds, the face soft above; the half-second is the moment the object DOES something — a part seating, a switch closing, a shape yielding.

refs.name: Threshold & Corridor Aspiration
refs.anchor: institutional-threshold grammar: a corridor, a doorway, a stair — the architecture of arriving somewhere, walked and not admired
refs.use: Grammar only — use for the arrival or transition beat. The building says 'this is a serious place' without a word of copy.
refs.avoid: NO drone-epic campus flyover; NO named real institutions, crests or logos; NO empty corridor as a symbol; NO golden-hour glow laid over everything; NO stock-photo group walking abreast at camera.
refs.dna: Photoreal education commercial still in the institutional-threshold lineage. Medium/era: contemporary premium promo, 24–35mm, locked or a slow forward drift. Named anchor: the corridor is walked, not photographed. Signature light: the corridor's own daylight ends — a window at the far wall, a stairwell above — pulling the eye down the length; overhead panels give the even base and no key is added. Color/grade: institutional neutrals with real material colour (terrazzo, painted steel, oak) and one warm value at the far light; no wash laid over the frame. Lens/optics: 24–35mm at a deep stop, camera at walking eye height, level and centred or deliberately offset — never a low hero angle. Texture/render: real floor wear at the traffic line, real scuff at the door edge, real notices on a real board; the building is in use. Composition+motion: strong one-point perspective running to the far light, a single figure entering the depth rather than posing in it; the half-second is a step — the figure crossing the threshold, the door not yet closed behind.

palettes.name: Native — World Default
palettes.bias: Inherits the selected world's palette_lock without modification; engage unless the brief demands a mood the native grade cannot serve.

palettes.name: Pastel Soft
palettes.bias: Sage-gray, peach-cream, blush-rose, warm white. High-key diffused north-window softness, all wrap, minimal depth contrast. NO true shadow, NO drama, NO saturated color.

palettes.name: Vibrant Education
palettes.bias: Navy, saffron-yellow, tomato-red, board-white. Broad saffron key lands flat and even, the navy ground drinks the falloff, one tomato beat punctuates, board-white bounces back clean. NO menace, NO muddy midtone, NO desaturation.

palettes.name: Deep Noir
palettes.bias: Photographic black, charcoal, wine-blood red, silver-gray. Total shadow absorption, one contained ember accent, industrial bounce barely lifting faces. NO ambient warmth, NO lifted shadow, NO saturated color.

palettes.name: Warm Autumn
palettes.bias: Umber, amber-tan, burnt-orange, golden cream. Grounded afternoon-sun warmth at every tonal stop, handmade material glow. NO cool tone, NO analytical distance, NO neon.

palettes.name: Cool Scientific
palettes.bias: Deep navy, teal-blue, bright cyan, cold aqua-white. Clinical blue-cyan precision, LED-readout accent, light-table clarity. NO warm element, NO earth tone, NO saturated primary.

palettes.name: Earth Natural
palettes.bias: Umber, tan-khaki, amber-gold, warm cream. Single consistent warm light source, restrained soil-and-sand naturalism. NO cool counter-tone, NO neon, NO high-contrast hierarchy.

palettes.name: High Contrast Bold
palettes.bias: Photographic black, saturated red, saturated yellow, offset white. Graphic two-primary tension, hard cel and poster separation, black-to-midtone cliff. NO transitional gradient, NO atmospheric light, NO desaturation.

palettes.name: Desaturated Cinematic
palettes.bias: Cool blue-gray, neutral gray, muted tan, warm-gray white. Overcast lifted-shadow restraint, one solitary muted-warm accent. NO saturated color, NO teal-orange split, NO warm-cozy lift.

palettes.name: Neon Rain Romance
palettes.bias: Violet-black, plum-purple, hot magenta, cyan-teal. Wet-neon night, hot-pink sign glow against a cool condensation halo. NO daylight, NO neutral light, NO desaturation.

palettes.name: Soviet Muted
palettes.bias: Sovcolor black, olive-gray, dim amber, sage-white. Photochemical institutional desaturation, one disproportionate dim-amber warmth signal. NO saturation, NO glamour light, NO teal-orange.

palettes.name: Golden Dust Epic
palettes.bias: Scorched brown, tan-brown, amber-gold, bone-cream. Low-angle desert-sun grazing, geologically-scaled warm monochrome. NO cool interruption, NO shadow lift, NO teal wash.

agents.name: Idea Generator
agents.summary: Chooses Production Path before scenario. Turns weak client briefs into routes, recommendation, scene architecture and MAMILAS handoff.

agents.name: Master Brief
agents.summary: Creates strict path-aware Final Brief, resolves path conflicts, protects Render Lock, source integrity, scene IDs and @tags.

agents.name: Image Director
agents.summary: Reads Production Path before World. Real path = photoreal/commercial/live-action frames. Animation path = tactile metaphor. Preserves @tags/logo/text.

agents.name: Motion Director
agents.summary: Pure motion only. Reads Production Path before camera. Real path = restrained filmic. Animation = material physics. 5-6s beat/split aware.

agents.name: Proof Director
agents.summary: Fails contamination, skipped IDs, weak render locks, text/logo risk, scene source loss, generic AI-video smell.

agents.name: Suno Music
agents.summary: Path-aware instrumental score, Custom Mode thinking, no vocals unless requested, VO-safe mix.

agents.name: Client Pitch
agents.summary: Turns Final Brief into client-facing concept rationale and approval text without exposing prompt mechanics.

golden.name: IMAGE · Product Hero · Nano Banana
golden.bad: A cinematic shot of a high-fidelity phone case, dramatic lighting, ultra realistic, 4K.
golden.why_bad: Generic. No geometry lock, logo/text rule, material behavior, lens, reflection, surface fidelity, start-frame stability.
golden.gold: Ultra-real high-fidelity commercial studio frame, exact product geometry locked, macro 100mm lens, clean black negative space, controlled gold rim light across aramid fiber texture, crisp edge highlights, realistic micro-scratches and weave depth, stable logo/packaging plane if present, shallow depth of field, product centered as hero object, no CGI plastic sheen, no warped text, no extra objects, frozen cinematic start frame ready for motion.

golden.name: IMAGE · Human Testimonial · Realism
golden.bad: A happy customer talking to camera in a nice office, cinematic.
golden.why_bad: Stock testimonial. No skin texture, eye-line, wardrobe fabric, practical light, human imperfection, location credibility.
golden.gold: Photoreal human portrait film frame, 50mm lens at eye height, natural skin texture with subtle pores and asymmetry, believable eye-line slightly off-camera, real wardrobe fabric with visible weave, soft practical key light from window left, muted office background with authentic depth, restrained color grade, human-scale framing, calm expression, no plastic skin, no fake smile, no beauty smoothing, frozen start frame.

golden.name: IMAGE · Education · Object-led
golden.bad: A Pixar classroom where kids learn about liquid measurements.
golden.why_bad: Too literal/classroom. No tactile metaphor, dominant lesson-object, motion hook.
golden.gold: high-fidelity 3D Pixar-quality animated feature frame inside a handcrafted soft-clay learning world, one oversized transparent measuring beaker as the dominant lesson-object, warm classroom sunlight, tactile rounded materials, measurement marks physically engraved on a wooden base, liquid surface paused halfway with a visible light reflection ready to move, foreground clay droplets leading the eye, clean readable composition, no flat worksheet, no random labels, motion-ready start frame.

golden.name: MOTION · Product Hero · Kling 3.0
golden.bad: Camera zooms in while light sweeps over the phone case.
golden.why_bad: Lazy zoom. No parallax, no physical reflection choreography, no start/end discipline, no tail hold.
golden.gold: Low macro dolly slides once along the black tabletop, a blurred foreground edge giving parallax; as it arrives a single gold rim highlight travels once across the aramid weave and lands on the logo plane, which stays fixed and readable. Product geometry locked, no shape change. The move completes by ~5s into a three-quarter hero hold with clean negative space and a faint specular shimmer. Negative field: morphing, warping logo/text, changing product shape, extra objects, flicker. For more coverage add a second short beat instead of stretching this one.

golden.name: MOTION · Animation Education · Kling 3.0
golden.bad: The camera slowly pushes in and the objects glow.
golden.why_bad: Generic AI-video. No material/world physics, no educational reveal, no environment reaction, no final frame logic.
golden.gold: Child-eye push toward the clay lesson table; the liquid surface rises in ONE clean physical move and stops exactly at the engraved mark, a single warm highlight settling on the glass. Beaker geometry, table, engraved text and the characters stay fixed; one clay droplet wobbles once from the motion. Main event done by ~5s, then a stable three-quarter final hold with the level locked at the mark. Negative field: morphing, warping engraved text, changing faces, opening mouths, extra liquid, flicker. This beat completes well within Kling 3.0's 10s window — for more VO coverage, chain a second approved beat rather than stretching this one.

golden.name: MOTION · Documentary Civic · Kling 3.0
golden.bad: Drone shot of the city with cinematic music and people smiling.
golden.why_bad: Stock civic montage. No human scale, no motivated camera, no authentic public texture.
golden.gold: Handheld micro-drift begins at child height beside a real sidewalk, following the chalk line as pedestrians pass naturally in soft background parallax. The camera does not fly; it moves like a documentary operator walking slowly, letting practical morning light reveal the civic texture of paving stones, storefront glass and passing shadows. by the late beat the line leads into a modest public square where children’s movement creates the reveal. Final hold: stable human-scale frame, no politician hero shot, no stock crowd, no artificial glow.

golden.name: SUNO · Documentary / Corporate · v5.5
golden.bad: cinematic inspiring cinematic music with emotional vocals.
golden.why_bad: Too trailer-like and vocals fight voice-over.
golden.gold: Narration-safe instrumental documentary score, 82 BPM, soft felt piano pulse, restrained warm strings, low sub texture, subtle brushed percussion, no vocals, no lead melody during dialogue, gentle rise at midpoint, final warm resolve, clean mix with voice-over space, custom mode style layers: documentary, civic warmth, restrained cinematic, human scale.

golden.name: SUNO · Education · v5.5
golden.bad: Happy fun cheerful uplifting kids learning music.
golden.why_bad: Mood-filler. No named instruments, no bpm/groove, no VO pocket, no scene-mapped arc — Suno drifts.
golden.gold: Narration-safe instrumental education motif, 96 BPM light swing, felted celesta lead with a marimba answer, pizzicato string pulse, one soft clarinet, gentle shaker (no kick clipping the VO); clear midrange pocket left open for the narration VO and ducked under dialogue; intro one curious celesta phrase, build adds marimba and pizzicato at the concept reveal, one small peak on the transformation beat, warm resolve on a single held celesta note. Custom mode style layers: children's education, warm, curious, tactile, hummable. Exclude: vocals, lyrics, big drums, EDM drops, trailer brass, busy percussion.

golden.name: SUNO · Product Hero · v5.5
golden.bad: Luxury cinematic high-fidelity powerful emotional product music.
golden.why_bad: Trailer cliché. No instruments, no bpm, no space, no designed reveal sound, fights the VO.
golden.gold: Narration-safe high-fidelity product bed, 84 BPM, muted felt-piano pulse over a low sub-bass foundation, one designed signature synth tone reserved only for the product reveal, wide clean room; midrange pocket kept open for VO and ducked under dialogue; intro sparse pulse, build adds the sub at the macro pass, a single signature hit lands on the logo/reveal beat, clean resolve on one sustained low note. Custom mode style layers: high-fidelity commercial, restrained, confident, modern, spacious. Exclude: vocals, busy percussion clipping the VO, trailer brass, genre drift.

golden.name: PROOF · Reference IP Safety
golden.bad: Make the character look like Ichigo holding a Zanpakuto in Soul Society uniform.
golden.gold: FAIL. Reference contamination. Convert to: high-contrast black/orange blade-pressure grammar, negative-space slash timing, spiritual-energy abstraction, original character design, no Ichigo likeness, no Zanpakutō design, no uniform/logo/world copy.

golden.name: IMAGE · Game Art Direction · Zelda/Elden Mix
golden.bad: A Zelda and Elden Ring inspired fantasy world with a hero.
golden.why_bad: Names references but no translation; risks IP copy.
golden.gold: Original open-air mythic adventure frame: soft cel-shaded green-gold landscape meets decayed gothic ruin scale, low sun cutting through fogged stone arches, tiny original child explorer silhouette for scale, ancient-tech glow embedded in broken masonry, wide 24mm lens, clear foreground grass motion hook, no Link/Tarnished likeness, no Triforce, no armor/logo/weapon copy, IP-safe synthesis of exploration mood and mythic ruin gravity.

regression.name: Real Path Contamination
regression.input: Ultra Real Commercial + clay diorama + Pixar render lock
regression.expected: FAIL: real path cannot inherit clay/Pixar/diorama unless hybrid override is active.

regression.name: Source Loss
regression.input: Raw source 1200 chars, scene source 980 chars
regression.expected: FAIL: coverage below 100%; return to Lossless Ingest.

regression.name: Logo/Text Morph Risk
regression.input: Product hero with logo visible + motion prompt moves surface aggressively
regression.expected: FIX: freeze logo/text plane; only camera/light/reflection moves.

regression.name: Lazy Motion
regression.input: Camera slowly zooms in, lights glow, cinematic
regression.expected: FIX: add motivated camera arc, physical action, environment reaction, final tail hold.

regression.name: IP Reference Misuse
regression.input: Make it look like Luffy on the Thousand Sunny
regression.expected: FAIL/FIX: abstract into sunny ocean adventure, ship-deck scale, bold primaries; no Luffy/Sunny copy.

regression.name: Concept Monotony
regression.input: Brief with many CONCEPT lines but few unique subjects
regression.expected: FIX: too many scenes reuse the same concept; group source into thematic beats or deepen the concept bank.

regression.name: Fallback Leak
regression.input: Brief where multiple scenes use generic fallback concept templates
regression.expected: FAIL: generic fallback concepts repeated; expand EDU_SOURCE_BANK patterns or re-ingest source.

regression.name: Generic Bridge Subject
regression.input: Scene where primary subject is a generic placeholder instead of a concrete visual object
regression.expected: FIX: primary subject is a generic bridge phrase; replace with a concrete visible object from the source.
